briefing.headline: Rig up a Deep Diving Crankbait — steady spawn approach for stained water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Deep Diving Crankbait is a strong choice for spawn with stained water and stable conditions.

Robertson: When the swimbait bite slows and fish scatter, pick up a deep-diving crankbait like the SPRO Little John DD 70 to put the majority of your weight in the boat. It produced the bulk of a 32-pound bag.

Key adjustment for stable: moderate cadence — match the natural pace of local forage.

primaryApproach.retrieve: Robertson: When the swimbait bite slows and fish scatter, pick up a deep-diving crankbait like the SPRO Little John DD 70 to put the majority of your weight in the boat. It produced the bulk of a 32-p
primaryApproach.targets: Focus on flat, grass, point. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

alternateApproach.retrieve: Wheeler: Use 14-pound fluorocarbon to get the DT-20 crankbait down in current when fishing ledge fish
alternateApproach.targets: Focus on flat, grass, point. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

proInsights.insight: KVD: 'Don't assume you must finesse spawning bass. At the 2011 Classic, 4 of top 7 finishers power fished spinnerbaits and crankbaits on the same 150-yard spawning flat. Key conditions: hard bottom flat with stumps + grass, 10-degree water temp ri...

Robertson: When following postspawn bass down ditches with a deep diver, start by casting ahead and up on top, then bring the crankbait down the side of the ditch at about a 45-degree angle. The crankbait must hit bottom.
Wheeler: Casting angles are critical for deep cranking offshore structure - varying your angle of retrieve relative to the cover triggers reaction strikes from bigger fish.

briefing.depthStrategy: Stable spawn conditions have fish committed to beds in 1-5ft. Focus on hard-bottom areas in protected coves, pockets, and flats. Polarized glasses are essential for sight-fishing.
adjustIf: If conditions suddenly shift (pressure drop, wind change), be ready to speed up your approach — transitional moments produce big bites
If water clears significantly, downsize presentations and move to natural color patterns
If fish are short-striking, add a long pause after deflections to let the lure suspend
